L1.area: Smart contract compilation and bytecode processing infrastructure

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the bytecode compilation pipeline where a maliciously crafted smart contract declaration transaction can trigger an integer wraparound during bytecode decompression. Without runtime overflow detection, the wraparound leads to an infinite loop with high CPU usage, causing a denial-of-service condition for Starknet full-node implementations.

L3.area: Sierra-to-CASM smart contract bytecode compilation module
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the Sierra-to-CASM bytecode compilation pipeline where a maliciously crafted Declare v2/v3 transaction can trigger an integer wraparound during Sierra bytecode decompression. Without runtime overflow checks enabled in release builds, the integer wraps silently, causing an infinite loop with high CPU usage and resulting in a denial-of-service condition for Starknet full-node implementations.

ground_truth.reason: An integer overflow in the Sierra bytecode decompression logic allows remote attackers to trigger an infinite loop and high CPU usage by submitting a malicious Declare v2/v3 transaction, causing denial of service.
